stimulus: Mexican American chemist Mario Molina was a researcher and educator whose work helped to protect the atmosphere. Recognized in 1995 with the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for explaining the depletion of the ozone layer, ______blank led to international agreements that phased out the use of chlorofluorocarbons worldwide.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

answerOptions.content: Molina published research on chlorofluorocarbons in 1974, which
Molina published research on chlorofluorocarbons in 1974; it
chlorofluorocarbons were studied by Molina in 1974, and

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-modifier placement. This choice makes the noun phrase "Molina’s 1974 research" the subject of the sentence and places it immediately after the modifying phrase "Recognized...ozone layer." In doing so, this choice clearly establishes that Molina’s 1974 research—and not another noun in the sentence—was recognized in 1995 with the Nobel Prize in Chemistry.

Choice B is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier: placing "Molina" himself after the modifier illogically suggests that Molina (not his research) was recognized in 1995. Choice C is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier: placing "Molina" after the modifier illogically suggests that Molina was recognized in 1995. Choice D is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier: placing "chlorofluorocarbons" after the modifier illogically suggests that chlorofluorocarbons themselves were recognized in 1995.
